intro: You can request repeat prescriptions for chronic medication 24 hours a day (including weekends) in 2 ways:
patientenomgeving: You can request repeat prescriptions online via the patient environment on the website. You will need to log in first. Please note that after creating an account, it can take up to 7 days before the account is activated.
receptenlijn: Call your own GP's practice telephone number and select the prescription line option.
receptenlijnVragen: Name and date of birth of the person the prescription is for
Street and house number of your address
Name or names of the medicines you are ordering (keep the boxes handy!)
welkeMedicijnen:
You can only request prescriptions for medicines that you use on the advice of your GP or specialist chronically (usually daily).

If you want to request medicines for newly occurring complaints or temporary medicines -- which you were given for a certain period -- you cannot order these medicines not via the prescription line or website. For this, you can call the practice during business hours during the day, and if necessary your GP will be consulted about your request.
wanneerKlaar: If you leave a message on the prescription line or order a prescription via the website before 12:00 noon, you can 2 working days later collect your medicines from your own pharmacy (from 15:00 they will be ready).
wanneerKlaarVoorbeelden: If you call/email on Tuesday evening, your medicines will be ready at the pharmacy on Friday.
If you call/email on Friday afternoon after 12:00 or during the weekend, you can collect your medicines on Wednesday after 15:00.
